arguments.task: Open official PitchBook pages and extract evidence for: 1) one-line company/API description, 2) whether PitchBook has a public API and its type (REST, GraphQL, both, or none), 3) documented authentication methods on first-party developer/auth pages, 4) access/pricing or gating language for API/data access, 5) any official MCP/server documentation if it exists, 6) rate limit notes. Prefer canonical first-party pages such as developer, API, support, legal, or pricing/contact pages. Do not rely on search-result snippets. Read page text and return the exact URLs used with short evidence quotes or summaries for each field. If a page is JS-rendered or gated, note that clearly.

input.thought: Start browser research on official PitchBook sources and extract canonical evidence URLs.

raw_result.description: PitchBook offers direct access to its private and public market datasets through an API product for integrating PitchBook data into customer workflows and systems.
raw_result.auth_notes: From the gathered official-page evidence, PitchBook publicly states the API is a RESTful JSON API, but no first-party page text was gathered that documents a specific authentication method.
raw_result.gating_reason: The gathered official product pages describe PitchBook API as a product offering and PitchBook markets direct access data through product/contact flows rather than a self-serve developer signup or public free/trial API flow in the collected evidence.

api_surface.breadth: Official product-page evidence states PitchBook offers a RESTful API using JSON for direct access to PitchBook market data and workflows, but the collected evidence does not include endpoint-level developer documentation or schema breadth.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP or Model Context Protocol documentation was found in the gathered first-party evidence.

raw_result.blocker: Public first-party evidence gathered does not document usable API authentication or self-serve developer access; access appears sales-gated.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No public rate-limit documentation was found in the gathered first-party evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: The gathered official API product-page evidence indicates a RESTful JSON API and positions the offering as direct data access into customer systems, but not as an open self-serve developer platform.
raw_result.human_review_reason: A structured field cannot be fully supported from the gathered evidence: no first-party authentication documentation was captured, so auth_methods could not be verified from official page text.
